title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.title: Authentic Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) Recipe | Easy Homemade
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of making authentic Chinese Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) at home with our easy-to-follow recipe. Get fluffy buns and a crispy topping every time!
seo.h1: Authentic Chinese Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked authentic Chinese Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) with a golden, crispy topping and soft, fluffy interior.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the sweet, buttery goodness of homemade Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao), a beloved classic from Chinese bakeries.

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a quintessential treat from Hong Kong and Chinese bakeries, beloved for their unique contrast of a sweet, crumbly, cookie-like topping and a soft, fluffy bread base. Despite their name, these golden-domed buns contain no pineapple, but rather get their moniker from the topping's resemblance to a pineapple's textured skin. Enjoy them warm, often with a slice of butter tucked inside, for an unforgettable taste of comfort.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 300g all-purpose flour, 50g granulated sugar, 5g instant dry yeast, and 3g salt. Add 120ml lukewarm milk and 1 large egg. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface for 5-7 minutes until smooth. Incorporate 30g softened unsalted butter and knead for another 5-7 minutes until the dough is elastic and passes the windowpane test.

instructions.title: First Proofing
instructions.text: Place the kneaded dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size. This allows the yeast to activate and develop flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare the Topping
instructions.text: While the dough proofs, cream 60g softened unsalted butter and 60g granulated sugar together until light and fluffy. Beat in 1 large egg yolk. Gradually add 120g all-purpose flour, 10g milk powder (if using), and 2g baking powder (if using), mixing until a soft dough forms. Divide into 8 equal portions, flatten each into a disc, and refrigerate to firm up.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release air. Divide the dough into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Place them on a parchment-lined baking sheet, ensuring there is enough space between each bun for expansion.

instructions.title: Assemble and Second Proofing
instructions.text: Take a chilled topping disc from the refrigerator and carefully press it onto the top of each dough ball, gently stretching it to cover about two-thirds of the bun's surface. Use a knife to lightly score a crosshatch pattern onto the topping. Cover the buns loosely with plastic wrap and let them proof for another 30-45 minutes in a warm place until visibly puffy.

instructions.title: Bake the Buns
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Prepare an egg wash by whisking 1 egg yolk with 1 tsp water, then lightly brush the topping of each bun. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the buns are cooked through. Let cool slightly on a wire rack before serving.

tips: For the best windowpane test, stretch a small piece of dough; it should become thin enough to see through without tearing, indicating proper gluten development.
Ensure your butter for both dough and topping is softened, not melted, for the correct texture and easy incorporation.
Pineapple Buns are best enjoyed warm, fresh from the oven. For a traditional Hong Kong experience, slice them open and insert a cold slice of butter just before serving.
article:
Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao: A Sweet Taste of Chinese Bakery Heaven

Step into any bustling Chinese bakery, and your senses are immediately captivated by an array of delectable treats. Among them, one stands out with its distinctive golden, cracked crust and enticing aroma: the Pineapple Bun, or Bo Lo Bao (菠蘿包). This iconic pastry, a staple in Hong Kong-style bakeries worldwide, has garnered a cult following for its unique combination of textures and flavors. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that perfect balance of soft, fluffy bread and a sweet, crumbly topping in your own kitchen, you've come to the right place. Get ready to embark on a delightful baking journey to master the art of the Pineapple Bun.

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

The name "Pineapple Bun" often leads to a common misconception: does it contain pineapple? Surprisingly, no! The name actually refers to the bun's appearance. Its signature golden, craggily, and often slightly caramelized topping strikingly resembles the rough, textured skin of a pineapple. This ingenious visual trick has made the Pineapple Bun instantly recognizable and beloved.

At its heart, the Bo Lo Bao is a simple yet extraordinary sweet bun. It comprises two main components: a wonderfully soft, enriched bread dough underneath, and a crisp, sugary, cookie-like crust on top. The magic lies in this contrast – the tender, slightly sweet interior providing a comforting foundation, while the buttery, crumbly exterior delivers a delightful crunch and burst of sweetness. It's a testament to the brilliance of Chinese baking, offering a textural and flavor experience that is truly unique. Originating from Hong Kong, it's a popular breakfast item, afternoon snack, or accompaniment to milk tea, cherished by generations.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Pineapple Bun requires attention to detail and fresh ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need for both the dough and that essential crust:

For the Enriched Bread Dough:
*   3 cups (360g) High-protein bread flour (or all-purpose flour for a slightly softer crumb)
*   1/4 cup (50g) Granulated sugar
*   1 teaspoon (5g) Salt
*   2 teaspoons (6g) Instant dry yeast
*   1/2 cup (120ml) Warm milk (around 105-115°F / 40-46°C)
*   1 large Egg, beaten
*   1/4 cup (60g) Unsalted butter, softened
*   2-3 tablespoons (30-45ml) Warm water (if needed)

For the Pineapple Topping (Bo Lo Bao Crust):
*   1/2 cup (113g) Unsalted butter, cold and cubed
*   1/2 cup (100g) Granulated sugar
*   1 large Egg yolk
*   1 1/2 cups (180g) All-purpose flour
*   1/2 teaspoon Baking powder (optional, for extra crispness)
*   1-2 tablespoons Milk (if needed for consistency)

For Egg Wash:
*   1 large Egg, beaten with 1 tablespoon water

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Let’s get baking! Follow these steps carefully to create your delicious Pineapple Bun.

1.  **Prepare the Dough**: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, and salt. In a separate bowl, combine the warm milk, beaten egg, and yeast. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
2.  **Knead the Dough**: Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for about 10-15 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. Incorporate the softened butter gradually, continuing to knead until the butter is fully absorbed and the dough passes the windowpane test (stretching a small piece thin enough to see through without tearing).
3.  **First Proofing**: Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.
4.  **Prepare the Topping**: While the dough proofs, make the pineapple topping. In a medium bowl, cream together the cold cubed butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk until well combined. Gradually add the flour and baking powder (if using), mixing until a crumbly dough forms. If the mixture is too dry, add milk one tablespoon at a time until it comes together. Form the topping into a disk, wrap in plastic, and chill in the refrigerator.
5.  **Shape the Buns**: Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air. Divide the dough into 10-12 equal portions (about 60-70g each). Shape each portion into a smooth ball and place them on a parchment-lined baking sheet, spaced apart.
6.  **Shape the Topping**: Take the chilled topping dough and divide it into the same number of portions as your bread dough. Roll each portion into a thin disk (about 3-4 inches in diameter) or flatten it with your hands.
7.  **Assemble the Pineapple Buns**: Carefully place one topping disk over each proofed bread dough ball, gently pressing down to adhere it and letting it drape over the sides. The topping should cover about two-thirds of the bun.
8.  **Second Proofing**: Cover the assembled buns loosely with plastic wrap and let them rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until noticeably puffy.
9.  **Bake**: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Just before baking, brush the exposed dough (not the topping) of each Pineapple Bun with the egg wash. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and cracked, and the buns are cooked through.
10. **Cool**: Transfer the baked buns to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that signature crisp crust and fluffy interior for your Pineapple Bun takes a little finesse. Here are some pro tips:

*   **Don't Rush Proofing**: Patience is key for light and airy bread. Ensure your dough doubles in size during both proofing stages. A warm, humid environment (like a slightly warmed oven that’s turned off) can help.
*   **Kneading is Crucial**: Proper kneading develops the gluten, which gives the bread its structure and chewiness. Don't skimp on this step.
*   **Cold Butter for Topping**: Using cold, cubed butter for the topping is essential. It helps create that crumbly, cookie-like texture. If your butter is too warm, the topping might become greasy or spread too much.
*   **Don't Overmix the Topping**: Once the flour is added, mix just until combined. Overmixing develops gluten, which you don't want in a crumbly topping.
*   **Topping Placement**: Ensure the topping covers most of the bun, but don't try to wrap it completely underneath. The edges will naturally melt and spread, creating that iconic pineapple skin effect.
*   **Egg Wash**: Brushing the exposed bread dough with egg wash before baking gives the un-topped parts a beautiful golden sheen.
*   **Oven Temperature**: Make sure your oven is properly preheated. An accurate oven thermometer can be a baker's best friend.

## Serving Suggestions

There's nothing quite like a warm Pineapple Bun straight from the oven. The aroma alone is enough to make your mouth water.

*   **Classic "Bolo Yau"**: For the ultimate experience, slice a warm Pineapple Bun horizontally and insert a thick slab of cold, unsalted butter into the middle. The cold butter melts into the warm bun, creating an irresistible contrast of temperatures and a rich, creamy texture. This is known as "Bolo Yau" (菠蘿油) and is a must-try.
*   **Breakfast or Brunch**: Enjoy it with a cup of strong Hong Kong-style milk tea, coffee, or a glass of soy milk.
*   **Afternoon Snack**: A perfect pick-me-up any time of day.
*   **With Fillings**: While traditionally plain, some bakeries offer Pineapple Buns with a sweet custard or red bean paste filling, adding another layer of indulgence.

## Variations and Substitutions

Once you've mastered the classic Pineapple Bun, feel free to experiment with variations:

*   **Fillings**: Introduce a filling to the bread dough before the second proofing. Popular choices include sweet custard (nai wong bao), red bean paste, coconut cream, or even savory BBQ pork (char siu bao). You could even add a small dollop of pineapple jam for a truly "pineapple" bun experience!
*   **Topping Flavors**: Stir in a teaspoon of almond extract, vanilla extract, or even a pinch of cocoa powder into the topping mixture for subtle flavor changes.
*   **Glaze**: Instead of an egg wash on the exposed bread, you can brush the entire topping with a thin sugar glaze (powdered sugar mixed with a little milk) after baking for extra sweetness and shine.
*   **Healthier Alternatives**: While the traditional Pineapple Bun is a treat, you can experiment with whole wheat flour for a portion of the bread dough, or use less sugar in both the dough and topping for a slightly less sweet version. However, be aware that these changes will alter the texture and flavor profile.
*   **Vegan Options**: With vegan butter, plant-based milk, and egg substitutes (like flax eggs or commercial egg replacers), you can attempt a vegan version, though achieving the exact texture of the original might require some trial and error.

Making your own Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a rewarding experience that connects you to the rich tradition of Chinese baking. The delightful combination of soft bread and crunchy topping makes it a truly special pastry. So roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and prepare to bring a taste of the bustling Hong Kong bakery into your home. Happy baking!
